error_signature: Google Sheets 'Append row' drops rows under high concurrency
title: Google Sheets 'Append row' drops rows with high webhook load
symptom: When sending multiple webhook requests to append rows in Google Sheets, fewer rows are recorded than expected, with some requests resulting in missing rows.
not_this_if: Webhook requests are serialized or limited to one at a time
root_cause: Under high concurrency, multiple executions of the Google Sheets 'Append row' node may conflict, leading to overwriting or missing rows. The append operation does not guarantee unique entries when requests overlap significantly.
fix_steps: Pre-populate the Google Sheet with unique identifiers in a specific column (e.g., column B).
Change the Google Sheets node configuration from 'Append row' to 'Update'.
Set the matching column to the column containing unique identifiers (e.g., column B).
As requests come in, ensure they update the corresponding row based on the unique identifier.
agent_instruction: Pre-populate the Google Sheet with unique identifiers and switch to the 'Update' operation in the Google Sheets node.
